daily_summary: xAI launched Grok Voice Think Fast 1.0, a speed-optimized voice model for low-latency conversations. Separately, Anthropic signed a seven-year, $1.8 billion infrastructure deal with Akamai — the largest contract in Akamai’s 27-year history — to run Claude inference across 4,300 edge locations globally. Google also made Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite generally available at $0.25 per million input tokens, the fastest and cheapest model in the Gemini 3 series.

items.title: Greg Brockman highlights GPT-Realtime-2 powering live audio translation in browser
items.summary: OpenAI president Greg Brockman amplified a demo of GPT-Realtime-2 driving real-time audio translation inside Chrome — usable on YouTube, livestreams and meetings. The post signals continued investment in OpenAI's realtime voice stack and adjacent third-party agent integrations.

items.title: Sam Altman opens public feedback on next OpenAI flagship model
items.summary: Sam Altman asked X what users most want improved in the next OpenAI flagship, framing the post as part of the planning loop for the o-series successor. The thread drew thousands of responses and is being used as a soft signal for capability priorities.

items.title: FIS partners with Anthropic on AI for anti-money-laundering investigations
items.summary: Financial-services platform FIS announced a partnership with Anthropic to deploy Claude-powered agents for anti-money-laundering investigations and case triage. The pitch targets banks looking to compress AML alert backlogs while keeping human-in-the-loop adjudication.

items.title: NZ Public Service AI Toolkit adds policy template + AI records-management guidance
items.summary: New Zealand's digital.govt.nz refreshed its Public Service AI Toolkit with a customisable AI policy template for agencies and new guidance on records management for AI systems. The update aligns with MBIE's 2025 national AI strategy and gives public-sector teams a documented starting point for responsible AI adoption.

items.title: RBNZ flags advanced AI cyber risk to NZ borrowers and lenders
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of New Zealand says advanced AI models capable of automating cyber attacks could amplify operational risk to borrowers and regulated entities, threatening loan repayments. RBNZ is coordinating with peer agencies including the Reserve Bank of Australia to monitor the threat surface.

items.title: Grok Voice Think Fast 1.0
items.summary: xAI has released Grok Voice Think Fast 1.0, a new voice model designed for low-latency real-time conversational AI. The model is optimized for speed-first voice interactions where responsiveness matters more than extended reasoning, complementing xAI’s existing voice lineup.

items.title: How Alta Daily Uses Meta’s Segment Anything to Reimagine the Digital Closet
items.summary: Fashion startup Alta Daily has integrated Meta’s Segment Anything Model into its digital closet app, enabling one-click garment segmentation and background removal at production scale. The case study shows how SAM’s zero-shot segmentation generalises to consumer applications beyond the research setting.

items.title: Akamai surges on big LLM deal — Anthropic signs $1.8B edge-inference contract, largest in Akamai history
items.summary: Anthropic has signed a seven-year, $1.8 billion infrastructure deal with Akamai to run Claude inference across 4,300 edge locations in 130 countries — the largest contract in Akamai’s 27-year history. The deal reflects Anthropic’s strategy to distribute inference beyond hyperscale cloud providers; Akamai stock surged 26% on the announcement.

items.title: CyberSecQwen-4B: Why Defensive Cyber Needs Small, Specialized, Locally-Runnable Models
items.summary: CyberSecQwen-4B is a 4-billion parameter security-focused model from the AMD developer hackathon, optimized for local deployment on edge hardware. It demonstrates that small, specialized models can outperform larger general-purpose models on defensive cyber benchmarks without requiring cloud infrastructure or data exfiltration risks.

items.title: Improving Bash Generation in Small Language Models with Grammar-Constrained Decoding
items.summary: NVIDIA researchers demonstrate grammar-constrained decoding to substantially improve bash script generation in small language models, enabling reliable shell command output without fine-tuning on proprietary data. The approach enforces syntactic validity at inference time, cutting hallucinated flags and invalid pipelines.

items.title: EMO: Pretraining mixture of experts for emergent modularity
items.summary: AllenAI introduces EMO (Emergent Modularity via Pretraining), a mixture-of-experts approach where modules emerge naturally during pretraining without explicit routing supervision. The method shows improved specialisation and benchmark performance versus dense baselines.

items.title: Streaming Tokens and Tools: Multi-Turn Agentic Harness Support in NVIDIA Dynamo
items.summary: NVIDIA Dynamo now supports streaming tokens and multi-turn tool calls in agentic harnesses, allowing assistant reasoning turns to interleave with tool invocations and return structured results across conversation turns. This directly enables production-grade agent loops on Dynamo inference infrastructure.

items.title: See what happens when creative legends use AI to make ads for small businesses.
items.summary: Google launched The Small Brief, an initiative where ad industry veterans use Google AI to create breakthrough advertising campaigns for small local businesses, demonstrating AI-assisted creative production at the agency level.

items.title: OpenAI’s New GPT-5.5 Powers Codex on NVIDIA Infrastructure — and NVIDIA Is Already Putting It to Work
items.summary: NVIDIA has deployed OpenAI’s GPT-5.5 to power Codex across its own engineering workflows, with internal teams using the AI coding agent to accelerate software development at scale. The blog details how GPT-5.5’s enhanced agentic capabilities make it the backbone of Codex’s latest production release on NVIDIA’s infrastructure.

items.title: MedQA: Fine-Tuning a Clinical AI on AMD ROCm — No CUDA Required
items.summary: A hackathon team writes up fine-tuning a clinical question-answering model on AMD MI300X GPUs using ROCm, demonstrating a CUDA-free path for medical-domain LLMs. The post documents reproducible training scripts and a small evaluation harness for clinical tasks.

items.title: AI avatars to deliver real-time NZ Sign Language translations
items.summary: An NZ-led project is nearing completion on AI avatars that produce real-time New Zealand Sign Language translations, aimed at situations where a live interpreter is unavailable. The Deaf community has been involved in design, and trials are planned across public services.

items.title: NZ organisations accelerate AI adoption amid growing resilience and governance gaps
items.summary: A new report on New Zealand enterprises finds AI is being deployed faster than security and governance practices can keep up, especially in cybersecurity tooling and customer operations. The authors call for board-level AI risk frameworks and reporting standards.

items.title: Achieving Peak System and Workload Efficiency on NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 with Slurm Block Scheduling
items.summary: NVIDIA's GB200 NVL72 rack achieves peak efficiency via Slurm block scheduling that aligns GPU NVLink coherence zones with job boundaries, reducing cross-rack communication overhead for large training runs.

items.title: Model Quantization: Post-Training Quantization Using NVIDIA Model Optimizer
items.summary: NVIDIA Model Optimizer enables post-training quantization (PTQ) to run large models on consumer RTX GPUs with reduced VRAM and faster inference, covering INT8/INT4/FP8 recipes for popular architectures.

items.title: OpenAI previews GPT-5.5-Cyber for critical-infrastructure defenders
items.summary: OpenAI president Greg Brockman announced a limited preview of GPT-5.5-Cyber, a security-tuned variant of GPT-5.5 designed to help critical-infrastructure operators detect and patch code vulnerabilities. It ships with the Trusted Access for Cyber programme for vetted defenders.

items.title: Powering the Next American Century: US Energy Secretary Chris Wright and NVIDIA’s Ian Buck on the Genesis Mission
items.summary: US Energy Secretary Chris Wright and NVIDIA's Ian Buck discussed AI's role in powering America's energy future at the SCSP AI+ Expo, arguing that AI-driven demand requires and will accelerate new energy infrastructure investment.

items.title: Grok Imagine Quality Mode API
items.summary: xAI exposed Grok Imagine Quality Mode through the API, giving developers programmatic access to the higher-fidelity image generation tier previously limited to the Grok app. Pricing is per-image at the higher quality cost band.

items.title: Connectors now on Grok Web
items.summary: xAI rolled connectors out to Grok on the web, letting users link Gmail, Google Drive, GitHub, and other services so Grok can search and act across them. Mirrors agent-style integrations already shipped by ChatGPT and Claude.

items.title: Andrew Ng + CopilotKit: course on building UI-generating AI agents
items.summary: Andrew Ng announced a DeepLearning.AI short course with CopilotKit on building agents that generate dynamic UIs — charts, forms, whiteboards — directly inside chat. The course covers component-based, compositional, and third-party app integration patterns.

items.title: Scale AI launches SWE Atlas Refactoring Leaderboard — Claude Opus 4.7 leads
items.summary: Scale AI published a new SWE Atlas Refactoring leaderboard scoring how well coding agents perform multi-file refactors on real repos. Claude Code with Opus 4.7 currently tops the board, followed by Codex and GPT-5 variants.

items.title: Real-Time Performance Monitoring and Faster Debugging with NCCL Inspector and Prometheus
items.summary: NVIDIA describes NCCL Inspector, a Prometheus-compatible tool for live-monitoring NVIDIA Collective Communications Library traffic across multi-GPU training jobs. It surfaces stragglers and failed all-reduces faster than log-scraping, useful for teams running large distributed training.

items.title: SAM 3.1: Faster and More Accessible Real-Time Video Detection and Tracking With Multiplexing and Global Reasoning
items.summary: Meta AI released SAM 3.1, an updated Segment Anything Model with faster real-time video detection and tracking, multiplexing support for concurrent streams, and global reasoning that improves accuracy on complex scene segmentation.

items.title: DeepMind: AlphaEvolve Gemini-powered coding agent accelerating quantum, biotech, logistics
items.summary: Google DeepMind highlighted AlphaEvolve, a Gemini-powered coding agent that iteratively proposes and tests algorithm changes. They credit it with measurable speedups in quantum simulation, protein workflows, and logistics scheduling.

items.title: Introducing Muse Spark: Scaling Towards Personal Superintelligence
items.summary: Meta AI unveiled Muse Spark, positioned as a step toward personal superintelligence — an AI system designed to act as a persistent personal agent that scales with individual context, goals, and workflows over time.

items.title: OpenAI expands ChatGPT ads pilot to New Zealand, UK, Japan, South Korea
items.summary: OpenAI is broadening its ChatGPT advertising pilot to New Zealand, the UK, Japan, South Korea, and Canada/Australia, targeting Free and Go plan users with sponsored answers. It marks the first time ads will appear inside ChatGPT for NZ-based free users.

items.title: Parloa builds service agents customers want to talk to
items.summary: OpenAI case study on Parloa using its Realtime API to run multilingual phone-and-chat customer-service agents at scale for enterprises. Parloa reports faster handoffs and higher containment when GPT-class voice models replace legacy IVR.

items.title: Grok 4.1 Fast and Agent Tools API
items.summary: xAI released Grok 4.1 Fast, an optimised fast-inference variant of its Grok 4 model, along with an Agent Tools API enabling developers to build agentic pipelines with Grok's reasoning and tool-use capabilities.

items.title: Advancing voice intelligence with new models in the API
items.summary: OpenAI shipped GPT-Realtime-2 with GPT-5-class reasoning for low-latency voice agents, plus a faster Whisper variant and a new Realtime-Translate model covering 70 source languages and 13 outputs. The release targets production voice agents in the API and presages a ChatGPT Voice upgrade.

items.title: Commvault warns ANZ organisations are scaling AI faster than they can secure it
items.summary: A Commvault report says Australian and New Zealand organisations are deploying AI faster than they can build resilience or governance for it, with model and data backup, lineage, and rollback emerging as gaps. Practitioners are urged to extend disaster recovery plans to AI assets.

items.title: NCSC and Five Eyes cyber agencies warn channel partners over agentic AI risks
items.summary: New Zealand's NCSC joined Five Eyes counterparts in a joint advisory urging IT channel partners to harden controls before deploying agentic AI for clients. The guidance flags identity, action-scope, and audit-trail gaps as the highest-risk areas.

items.title: AI factory race reaches New Zealand: Datagrid plans build near Invercargill
items.summary: Singapore-based Datagrid is planning what would be New Zealand's first dedicated AI training data centre near Invercargill, leveraging Southland power. Coverage flags broad questions over grid impact, water use, and economic returns.

items.title: Government invests in Aimer Farming AI for pasture decisions
items.summary: MPI is co-funding Aimer Farming's AI platform that recommends rotational grazing decisions from pasture imagery and weather data. The deal is one of the few NZ government commitments to applied AI in primary production.

items.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite is now generally available
items.summary: Google has made Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite generally available at $0.25 per million input tokens, with 2.5× faster time-to-first-token and 45% faster output speeds than Gemini 2.5 Flash. It achieves 86.9% on GPQA Diamond and is Google’s fastest and most cost-effective production model, with built-in thinking levels across AI Studio and Vertex AI.

items.title: vLLM V0 to V1: Correctness Before Corrections in RL
items.summary: ServiceNow-AI on Hugging Face documents subtle numerical drift between vLLM V0 and V1 that silently broke RL training reward signals. The post argues teams should validate inference parity before tuning RL hyperparameters when migrating engines.

items.title: Higher usage limits for Claude and a compute deal with SpaceX
items.summary: Anthropic signed a deal with SpaceX to use all capacity at Colossus 1 in Memphis — 300MW, over 220,000 NVIDIA GPUs including H100, H200, and GB200s. This directly doubles Claude Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise five-hour rate limits, removes peak-hour throttling, and opens discussion of orbital AI compute at multiple gigawatts.

items.title: Accelerating Gemma 4: faster inference with multi-token prediction drafters
items.summary: Google released Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) drafters for the Gemma 4 family, delivering up to 3x faster inference without quality loss. A lightweight drafter predicts multiple tokens ahead while the larger model verifies them in parallel using speculative decoding; available under Apache 2.0 with support for Hugging Face, vLLM, SGLang, and Ollama.

items.title: xAI: New Compute Partnership with Anthropic
items.summary: xAI announced a compute partnership with Anthropic — the two frontier labs will share infrastructure, with xAI providing Anthropic with access to its training capacity. The deal is unusual given the labs' otherwise opposed positioning on AI safety, and signals how acute compute scarcity is for every frontier player.

items.title: Scaling How We Build and Test Our Most Advanced AI
items.summary: Meta AI describes its internal infrastructure and methodology for building and evaluating its most advanced models, covering automated red-teaming, benchmark construction, and scaling evaluation pipelines to match model capability growth.

items.title: Introducing Claude Design by Anthropic Labs
items.summary: Anthropic Labs launched Claude Design, a product for creating polished visual outputs — designs, prototypes, slides, and one-pagers — through collaboration with Claude. It sits alongside Claude Code as a Labs-track product for non-coding creative workflows.

items.title: Enabling Claude Code to work more autonomously
items.summary: Anthropic introduced Routines in Claude Code — async automations that let developers set up overnight pipelines and wake to pull requests ready to merge. Code Review is now built in and used by Anthropic's own teams. The redesigned desktop app adds session sidebar, drag-and-drop workspace, integrated terminal, SSH support, and faster diffs.

items.title: Claude for Creative Work
items.summary: Anthropic launched connectors linking Claude to Adobe, Blender, Autodesk Fusion, Ableton, Resolume, Affinity by Canva, SketchUp, and Splice — plus art school partnerships at RISD, Ringling, and Goldsmiths. Designers can now create and modify 3D models, batch-automate production tasks, and control VJ tools through natural language.

items.title: NVIDIA Spectrum-X — the Open, AI-Native Ethernet Fabric — Sets the Standard for Gigascale AI Factories
items.summary: NVIDIA detailed Spectrum-X Ethernet as the scale-out fabric for gigascale AI training clusters, with the new Multipath Reliable Connection (MRC) protocol developed with AMD, Broadcom, Intel, Microsoft, and OpenAI. The pitch is that Ethernet, hardened with MRC and Spectrum-X switches, can match InfiniBand for AI factories that approach a million accelerators.

items.title: Introducing Grok Business and Grok Enterprise
items.summary: xAI launched Grok Business and Grok Enterprise subscription tiers, bringing Grok's AI capabilities to professional and enterprise customers with higher rate limits, team management, and priority access to new models.

items.title: Adding Benchmaxxer Repellant to the Open ASR Leaderboard
items.summary: Hugging Face is hardening its Open ASR Leaderboard against benchmark gaming by introducing a private holdout split. Speech-recognition models will now be evaluated on data they cannot have trained on, reducing the value of overfitting to public test sets and giving more credible relative rankings.

items.title: OpenAI: How frontier enterprises are building an AI advantage
items.summary: OpenAI published a B2B Signals research note on how leading enterprises deepen AI adoption. The piece argues frontier customers are scaling Codex-powered agentic workflows beyond pilot use and treating durable agent infrastructure — not one-shot prompts — as the source of competitive advantage.

items.title: AI Blueprint for Aotearoa: a refreshed vision to 2030
items.summary: New Zealand's AI Blueprint Working Group launched a refreshed national AI roadmap targeting global AI leadership by 2030. Six priority sectors: agriculture, AEC, creative industries, education, environment, and health. Key finding: AI uptake is growing but trust is not keeping pace with usage.

items.title: Introducing ChatGPT Futures: Class of 2026
items.summary: OpenAI launched ChatGPT 26, seeking 26 students and recent graduates already building with AI. The cohort — the first to go through college with ChatGPT — gets invited to OpenAI HQ in June 2026 for a summit; applications closed April 5.

items.title: NVIDIA and ServiceNow Partner on New Autonomous AI Agents for Enterprises
items.summary: NVIDIA and ServiceNow are partnering to deliver autonomous AI agents into enterprise environments, combining NVIDIA's AI infrastructure with ServiceNow's workflow platform. The collaboration targets agentic AI deployment at scale, moving beyond simple prompts to multi-step task execution inside enterprise systems.

items.title: Google is partnering with XPRIZE and Range Media Partners on the $3.5 million Future Vision film competition.
items.summary: Google announced a partnership with XPRIZE and Range Media Partners on a $3.5 million Future Vision film competition. The contest invites filmmakers to create works that imagine positive AI futures, with Google providing AI tooling for entrants.

items.title: How to Build In-Vehicle AI Agents with NVIDIA: From Cloud to Car
items.summary: NVIDIA's developer blog provides a technical walkthrough of building multimodal agentic AI for automotive cockpits, covering the full cloud-to-car architecture. The guide addresses how in-vehicle agents can reason, plan, and respond across voice, vision, and sensor inputs using NVIDIA's DriveOS stack.

items.title: Building for the Rising Complexity of Agentic Systems with Extreme Co-Design
items.summary: NVIDIA argues that as AI systems shift from single-model inference to multi-agent coordination, hardware, software, and AI model design must be co-designed together — not built in isolation. The post outlines an 'extreme co-design' framework for scaling agentic system complexity without compounding failure modes.

items.title: Four MTIA Chips in Two Years: Scaling AI Experiences for Billions
items.summary: Meta has shipped four generations of its MTIA (Meta Training and Inference Accelerator) chips in two years, scaling custom silicon to serve AI features for billions of users across Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp.

items.title: The Future of AI Is Open and Proprietary
items.summary: NVIDIA makes the case that the AI ecosystem will remain a mix of open-weight and proprietary models rather than one winning, noting that both strategies serve different enterprise needs and that open models accelerate community innovation while proprietary ones enable differentiated products.

items.title: Anthropic: Agents for financial services and insurance
items.summary: Anthropic released 10 preconfigured Claude agent templates for banks, asset managers, and insurers: pitchbooks, earnings analysis, credit memos, underwriting, KYC, month-end close, and insurance claims. Integrates with Moody's, Dun & Bradstreet, and Microsoft 365; Claude scored 88% accuracy on insurance claims versus human experts.

items.title: Supporting the DOW's mission with AI
items.summary: xAI announced it is supporting the US Department of Defense with AI capabilities, marking a significant government contract for the company and adding it to the list of frontier AI labs working with US defence agencies.

items.title: GPT-5.5 Instant System Card
items.summary: OpenAI published the system card for GPT-5.5 Instant, documenting capabilities, evaluations, and safety testing for the new variant. The card details performance benchmarks and the model's positioning relative to other GPT-5 variants.

items.title: GPT-5.5 Instant: smarter, clearer, and more personalized
items.summary: OpenAI has replaced ChatGPT's default model with GPT-5.5 Instant, which delivers 52.5% fewer hallucinations than GPT-5.3 Instant on high-stakes prompts across medicine, law, and finance. The model gains personalization by referencing past conversations, files, and Gmail — initially for Plus and Pro users — and ships to all ChatGPT tiers.

items.title: NZ firms warned overseas businesses aren't getting a return on AI - MPs report
items.summary: A NZ Parliamentary report cautions local businesses that overseas AI investments often fail to generate returns, while still encouraging domestic adoption. The report references international evidence showing many enterprise AI deployments underperform on ROI.

items.title: Massey University study: generative AI is replacing entry-level creative roles in NZ
items.summary: A Massey University pilot study finds generative AI is displacing junior roles in New Zealand creative industries, with productivity gains often captured by overseas firms rather than local agencies.

items.title: Five Eyes guidance: don't give agentic AI the keys to sensitive data
items.summary: Five Eyes intelligence agencies (US, UK, Canada, Australia, NZ) issued joint guidance urging organisations to limit agentic AI access to sensitive data. The guidance is directly relevant to NZ insurers and government agencies considering autonomous workflows.

items.title: OpenAI and PwC collaborate to reimagine the office of the CFO
items.summary: OpenAI and PwC have announced a partnership to deploy AI agents across enterprise finance functions, targeting forecasting, reporting, and internal controls. The collaboration positions AI agents as the primary interface for CFO-level decision workflows, building on PwC's existing enterprise client base.

items.title: Optimize Supply Chain Decision Systems Using NVIDIA cuOpt Agent Skills
items.summary: NVIDIA's cuOpt library has introduced 'Agent Skills' — modular AI capabilities that let agents autonomously solve supply chain optimization problems including vehicle routing, scheduling, and capacity allocation. The post demonstrates integrating cuOpt skills into agent pipelines for real-time decision support under volatile demand.

items.title: BNZ books a $352m write-down as AI accelerates legacy software obsolescence
items.summary: Bank of New Zealand recorded a $352 million write-down on legacy systems, citing AI-driven advancements that are rapidly aging existing software and lifting upgrade costs. The disclosure is the first material AI-related impairment in NZ banking and may foreshadow similar moves at peers.

items.title: Beca uses Microsoft Azure and GPT-5.1 to add AI to New Zealand Geospatial Data platform
items.summary: Engineering firm Beca, working with Microsoft, integrated Azure OpenAI's GPT-5.1 into its BEYON system to add AI capabilities to the New Zealand Geospatial Data platform. The deployment focuses on natural-language querying of national infrastructure datasets.

items.title: The latest AI news we announced in April 2026
items.summary: Google's monthly AI updates recap for April 2026, covering Gemini 3.1 Ultra launch with 2M-token context window, Veo 3 video generation advances, and integrations across Google Workspace and Search.

items.title: Cisco acquires Astrix Security for ~$400M to monitor AI agent permissions
items.summary: Cisco is acquiring Israeli startup Astrix Security, an Anthropic and Menlo Ventures-backed company that monitors and controls permissions granted to AI agents. The deal underscores how managing autonomous-agent identities is becoming a discrete enterprise security category.

items.title: Katie Haun closes $1B across two funds to back AI agent and financial-services startups
items.summary: Former Andreessen Horowitz partner Katie Haun raised $1 billion across two funds to invest in early- and later-stage companies at the intersection of financial services, AI, and alternative assets. The expansion beyond crypto reflects renewed conviction in agentic AI applications.

items.title: Sierra raises $950M Series E at $15.8B valuation for AI customer-service agents
items.summary: Bret Taylor's Sierra closed $950 million in Series E funding led by Tiger Global and GV at a $15.8 billion valuation, up from $10 billion in 2025. The company builds AI agents for customer service and reports rapid enterprise adoption.

items.title: Anthropic announces $1.5B joint venture with Blackstone, Goldman Sachs, and Hellman & Friedman to deploy Claude across PE-backed companies
items.summary: Anthropic unveiled a $1.5 billion joint venture with Blackstone, Goldman Sachs, and Hellman & Friedman to industrialise Claude deployments across the firms' private-equity portfolios. The JV targets workflow rebuilds in customer support, finance, and coding rather than raw model access, signalling a shift toward managed enterprise AI deployment.

items.title: Compute Optimal Tokenization research sweeps tokenizers, params, and data across LLM compute budgets
items.summary: Tomasz Limisiewicz announced "Compute Optimal Tokenization" research, sweeping tokenizers, model parameters, and training data across compute budgets to derive Chinchilla-style guidance for tokenizer choice. The work suggests tokenizer design is an underappreciated lever for compute efficiency at frontier scale.

items.title: OpenAI launches $10B "Deployment Company" JV with TPG and SoftBank to industrialise AI rollouts
items.summary: OpenAI is forming a $10 billion joint venture with private-equity firms including TPG and SoftBank to deploy AI across roughly 2,000 portfolio companies. The vehicle mirrors Anthropic's smaller PE deal and emphasises selling end-to-end workflow transformation rather than API access.

items.title: From RTX to Spark: NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI
items.summary: NVIDIA demonstrates running Google's Gemma 4 locally on RTX GPUs via the RTX AI Garage and Project Spark, enabling local agentic AI workflows without cloud dependency — relevant for on-device agent deployment.

items.title: xAI and El Salvador Pioneer the World's First Nationwide AI Education Program
items.summary: xAI and El Salvador announced a partnership to roll out the world's first nationwide AI education program, integrating Grok into the country's school curriculum to provide personalised AI-assisted learning at scale.

items.title: DeepClaude — Claude Code agent loop integrated with DeepSeek V4 Pro
items.summary: DeepClaude pairs Claude Code's agent loop with DeepSeek V4 Pro for code generation, attempting to combine Claude's tool-use orchestration with a cheaper Chinese open-weights backend. The project trended on Hacker News with 600+ points.

items.title: NIST CAISI evaluation: DeepSeek V4 Pro trails leading US AI models by about eight months
items.summary: NIST's Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI) assessed DeepSeek V4 Pro as the most capable Chinese model to date but found it lags top US systems by roughly eight months on capability benchmarks. The evaluation is the first formal US government performance gap measurement for a Chinese frontier model.

items.title: OpenAI employees raise alarm over ChatGPT's failure to escalate violence threats to law enforcement
items.summary: Internal OpenAI staff, per WSJ reporting, flagged that ChatGPT failed to escalate or alert authorities when users described real-world violence plans, including a recent mass shooting case. The disclosures highlight gaps in OpenAI's safety review and incident-response protocols for users disclosing imminent harm.

items.title: Palo Alto Networks to acquire AI gateway startup Portkey for $120M-$140M
items.summary: Palo Alto Networks is buying AI infrastructure startup Portkey for an estimated $120M-$140M, expanding its security stack into the AI gateway category that brokers, observes, and rate-limits LLM calls for enterprise agent deployments. Portkey was previously backed by Elevation Capital.

items.title: NZ Maritime Operations Centre upgrade adds AI for alarm consolidation and monitoring
items.summary: Wellington's Maritime Operations Centre is rolling out an AI layer that consolidates the hundreds-to-thousands of alarms operators face into the two or three that actually need attention, per Kordia CEO Neil Livingston. He framed the system as assisting rather than replacing human operators in safety-critical maritime monitoring.

items.title: Chat with Qwen (with Deep Research and Web Dev)
items.summary: Qwen launched a public chat interface with Deep Research and Web Dev modes, exposing its agentic research and code-generation capabilities directly to end users via chat.qwen.ai.

items.title: NCSC-NZ + Five Eyes issue joint guidance on careful adoption of agentic AI
items.summary: New Zealand's National Cyber Security Centre, alongside Five Eyes partners, released joint guidance on agentic AI deployment. The document warns that LLM-powered agents inherit prompt-injection and data-poisoning risks and outlines security expectations across sectors.

items.title: Pentagon strikes AI deals with seven big tech firms after shunning Anthropic
items.summary: The US Department of Defense awarded AI contracts to seven major tech vendors but excluded Anthropic, reportedly due to Anthropic's restrictions on military use. The deals widen access to frontier models for defence applications.

items.title: Anthropic opens Sydney office, names Theo Hourmouzis GM for Australia and New Zealand
items.summary: Anthropic has formalised its Australia/New Zealand presence with a Sydney office and ex-Snowflake VP Theo Hourmouzis as General Manager. The move signals deeper enterprise sales motion in the region, alongside partnerships such as TrendAI's Claude Opus 4.7 deployment.

items.title: OpenAI to expand ChatGPT ads pilot to Australia, New Zealand and Canada
items.summary: OpenAI is broadening its ChatGPT advertising pilot beyond the US into Australia, New Zealand and Canada in the coming weeks. Updated privacy terms now allow user data sharing with advertisers for targeted placements.

items.title: TrendAI deepens Anthropic partnership using Claude Opus 4.7 for vulnerability research
items.summary: TrendAI expanded its Anthropic partnership, using Claude Opus 4.7 to power agentic vulnerability research workflows. Marketed as a regional AI-security play tied to Anthropic's new A/NZ presence.

items.title: Custom Voices and Voice Library
items.summary: xAI has launched custom voice creation and a Voice Library for Grok, enabling users to design personalised AI voices and select from curated voice options for text-to-speech and interactive applications.

items.title: RBNZ: Assessing AI and Robotics Exposure in the New Zealand Labour Market Using LLMs
items.summary: Reserve Bank of New Zealand published research using LLMs to measure AI and robotics exposure across NZ occupations. Highest exposure falls on professional, managerial, and administrative roles at middle-to-upper income levels; methodology maps task-level vulnerability using LLM-based scoring rather than prior O*NET-style surveys.

items.title: Nemotron Labs: What OpenClaw Agents Mean for Every Organization
items.summary: By early 2026, the open-source OpenClaw agent project crossed 100,000 GitHub stars. NVIDIA's Nemotron Labs examines how OpenClaw's modular, composable approach to autonomous AI agents can be applied across enterprise workflows, from supply chain orchestration to customer operations.

items.title: Andrew Ng launches "AI Prompting for Everyone" course
items.summary: Andrew Ng announced a new course teaching prompting techniques applicable across ChatGPT, Gemini, and Claude. Covers deep research, attaching docs and images for context, and developing intuition for model behaviour.

items.title: NVIDIA ships TensorRT for RTX runtime to speed up Unreal Engine NNE inference
items.summary: NVIDIA released a TensorRT for RTX runtime integration for Unreal Engine's Neural Network Engine (NNE), letting game developers run on-device neural inference for graphics, animation, and AI gameplay using the same engine path. The runtime targets the consumer RTX install base for shipped titles.

items.title: Build AI-Powered Games with NVIDIA DLSS 4.5, RTX, and Unreal Engine 5
items.summary: Today, game developers can begin integrating NVIDIA DLSS 4.5 with Dynamic Multi Frame Generation, Multi Frame Generation 6X, and the second-generation...

items.title: How to Build, Run, and Scale High-Quality Creator Workflows in ComfyUI
items.summary: NVIDIA details production-ready patterns for deploying ComfyUI at scale — covering GPU resource management, workflow orchestration, and quality controls for teams running image and video generation pipelines in enterprise environments.

items.title: Automating GPU Kernel Translation with AI Agents: cuTile Python to cuTile.jl
items.summary: NVIDIA demonstrates using AI agents to automatically translate GPU kernels from cuTile Python to cuTile.jl, enabling cross-language GPU portability. The approach reduces manual porting effort and could generalise to other language pairs in the CUDA ecosystem.

items.title: OpenAI rolls out GPT-5.5-Cyber for critical cybersecurity defenders
items.summary: Sam Altman announced the rollout of GPT-5.5-Cyber, a cybersecurity-focused model for critical defenders, with ecosystem and government collaboration. The post drew over 10k likes and 696k views, signalling significant interest in domain-specialised frontier models.

items.title: Logan Kilpatrick: Google "pushing Gemini full gas" against OpenAI hype
items.summary: Google AI lead Logan Kilpatrick said the team is "pushing Gemini full gas," responding to the wave of attention around OpenAI announcements this week. The comment came alongside the GPT-5.5 rollout and Musk-OpenAI trial coverage.

items.title: Enabling a new model for healthcare with AI co-clinician
items.summary: Google DeepMind publishes research on an AI co-clinician framework, exploring how AI can augment clinical decision-making while keeping human oversight central. The paper outlines evaluation criteria and an architecture for AI-assisted care intended for real-world deployment.

items.title: Chollet: AI automates tasks, not jobs — cheaper tasks expand demand
items.summary: François Chollet argues that AI is automating individual tasks rather than wholesale jobs — citing translators and customer support reps as examples where end-to-end automation has not arrived. He notes that cheaper tasks historically increase demand for the surrounding work, citing radiologists.

items.title: OpenAI DevDay 2026 announced for September 29 in San Francisco
items.summary: OpenAI confirmed DevDay 2026 will be held on September 29 in San Francisco, the company's flagship developer event where new APIs and models are typically unveiled.

items.title: Grok Speech to Text and Text to Speech APIs
items.summary: xAI launches Speech-to-Text and Text-to-Speech APIs for Grok, enabling developers to add voice input and output to applications via the Grok API. This extends xAI's developer platform toward multimodal voice capabilities alongside the existing language and vision APIs.

items.title: Introducing Advanced Account Security
items.summary: OpenAI introduces Advanced Account Security with phishing-resistant login (passkeys/hardware keys), stronger account recovery, and enhanced protections against account takeover — particularly relevant for enterprise customers and API users managing sensitive workloads.

items.title: Where the goblins came from
items.summary: OpenAI explains the root cause of quirky 'goblin' personality artifacts in GPT-5 outputs, tracing how reinforcement learning fine-tuning phases can inadvertently introduce unintended model behaviours, and details the mitigations applied to resolve them.

items.title: AI evals are becoming the new compute bottleneck
items.summary: Hugging Face researchers argue that as model training costs fall, evaluation costs are emerging as the dominant compute bottleneck. Running rigorous evals at scale now consumes more compute than training for some use cases, prompting a rethink of eval efficiency, tooling, and benchmark design across the industry.

items.title: Powering AI Factories with NVIDIA Enterprise Reference Architectures
items.summary: NVIDIA publishes enterprise reference architectures for AI factories — validated hardware-software blueprints for deploying agentic AI systems at scale with reliability and performance guarantees. The designs target organisations building production AI infrastructure for reasoning, automation, and intelligent decision-making workloads.

items.title: Granite 4.1 LLMs: How They're Built
items.summary: IBM shares training details for the Granite 4.1 LLM series, covering architecture, dataset curation, and alignment techniques. The post emphasises reliability and factual accuracy for enterprise business applications, positioning Granite as a controllable, enterprise-safe alternative to frontier consumer models.

items.title: Google AI Studio adds multi-chat sessions and integrated Search Grounding for the Gemini API
items.summary: Google AI Studio now supports running parallel Gemini chat sessions side-by-side, and Search Grounding is integrated directly into the Gemini API. Logan Kilpatrick teased more launches before Google I/O.

items.title: Cybersecurity in the Intelligence Age
items.summary: OpenAI outlines a five-part action plan for strengthening cybersecurity using AI: democratising AI-powered cyber defence, protecting critical systems, combating adversarial AI use, building international norms, and partnering with governments. The post frames AI as both a threat vector and the primary defence tool for the intelligence era.

items.title: Google launches Grounding With Exa for Gemini — agentic web search across billions of pages, partnership with Exa Labs
items.summary: Google partnered with Exa Labs to ship "Grounding With Exa" inside Gemini, giving Gemini agentic web search across billions of indexed sites. Aimed at Gemini API developers building agents that need broad web retrieval.

items.title: DeepInfra on Hugging Face Inference Providers
items.summary: Hugging Face adds DeepInfra as an official inference provider, giving users direct access to a wide catalog of open models via DeepInfra's infrastructure from the HF hub. This extends the inference ecosystem alongside existing partners like Together AI and Fireworks AI, simplifying multi-provider model deployment.

items.title: TUANZ launches 2026 Digital Priorities Report: warns NZ innovation gap widening, urges national AI workforce framework
items.summary: TUANZ released its 2026 Digital Priorities Report calling on government to lead on AI worker training, mandatory cybersecurity standards treating AI as digital employees under zero-trust, a national digital clearing house, and national digital ID. Report warns NZ has dropped to 23rd in the Network Readiness Index and that "Shadow AI" is expanding the attack surface.

items.title: Musk testifies in OpenAI trial: 'it is not okay to steal a charity'; judge warns both sides on social media
items.summary: Elon Musk took the witness stand in the federal trial against OpenAI, arguing its nonprofit-to-for-profit conversion sets a dangerous precedent for charitable organisations. The presiding judge separately warned both Musk and Sam Altman to 'control your propensity to use social media to make things worse outside this courtroom'.

items.title: China unveils Lingshen supercomputer targeting 2+ exaFLOPS using domestic CPU-only architecture
items.summary: China's National Supercomputing Center in Shenzhen unveiled the Lingshen project, aiming for over 2 exaFLOPS of performance using exclusively domestically manufactured processors. The initiative is part of China's wider effort to build sovereign AI compute infrastructure independent of NVIDIA and other US suppliers.

items.title: NVIDIA launches Nemotron 3 Nano Omni: unified vision, audio, and language model with 9x agent throughput
items.summary: NVIDIA released Nemotron 3 Nano Omni, an open multimodal model that handles screens, documents, audio, video, and text within a single integrated reasoning loop. NVIDIA claims 9x higher throughput than comparable open omni models, targeting AI agent applications that previously required multiple fragmented model chains.

items.title: Scaling Biomolecular Modeling Using Context Parallelism in NVIDIA BioNeMo
items.summary: NVIDIA BioNeMo adopts context parallelism to scale biomolecular modeling across multiple GPUs, enabling longer protein sequences and more complex biological structures than single-GPU memory permits. The approach distributes attention computation across devices, pushing the frontier for AI-driven drug discovery workloads.

items.title: Sources: Apple plans on-device AI photo overhaul for iOS 27 — extend, enhance, and reframe using local models
items.summary: Apple is overhauling iOS 27's Photos app with on-device AI models capable of extending scene boundaries, enhancing image quality, and reframing compositions without cloud processing. The initiative targets competitive parity with Android's AI photography features while preserving privacy.

items.title: Claude.ai unavailable and elevated errors on the Anthropic API
items.summary: Anthropic's Claude.ai experienced an outage with elevated API error rates, surfacing on HackerNews with significant community discussion (232 points, 198 comments). Service disruption affecting both the consumer product and developer API.

items.title: South Africa withdraws its first AI policy draft after discovering it contained AI-generated fictitious sources
items.summary: South Africa withdrew its inaugural national AI policy draft after the reference list was found to contain fabricated citations that appeared to have been generated by an AI system. The incident underscores the risk of using AI tools in official government policy work without robust human verification.

items.title: Google drops out of $100M Pentagon challenge to build voice-controlled autonomous drone swarms after internal ethics review
items.summary: Google withdrew from a $100 million Pentagon competition to develop voice-controlled autonomous drone swarm technology following an internal ethics review. The decision comes the same day 600+ Google and DeepMind employees demanded the company reject classified DOD AI contracts entirely.

items.title: OpenAI models, Codex, and Managed Agents come to AWS
items.summary: OpenAI and Amazon announced an expanded partnership making OpenAI models, Codex, and Managed Agents available through AWS, one day after OpenAI revised its Microsoft partnership to allow multi-cloud distribution. Enterprises can now deploy secure AI in their AWS environments via Bedrock Managed Agents.

items.title: AWS launches Amazon Connect Decisions and Talent — agentic AI tools for logistics and recruiting
items.summary: AWS introduced two AI agent products: Amazon Connect Decisions for logistics workflow automation and Amazon Connect Talent for AI-assisted recruiting. The launches expand AWS's enterprise AI footprint alongside the OpenAI partnership announcement.

items.title: Lovable launches AI coding app on iOS and Android — build via voice or text from mobile
items.summary: Lovable released its no-code AI app builder on iOS and Android, enabling developers to create software using voice or text prompts on mobile. Users can switch seamlessly between mobile and desktop workflows, extending the vibe-coding trend beyond the browser.

items.title: FIDO Alliance launches working groups to set security standards for AI agent transactions; Google contributes Agent Payments Protocol
items.summary: The FIDO Alliance formed two working groups to develop industry standards for securing AI agent-initiated financial transactions, with Google contributing its Agent Payments Protocol. The initiative addresses the emerging risk of autonomous AI systems conducting purchases or transfers without adequate authentication.

items.title: Latent Space: ImageGen is on the path to AGI — GPT-Image-2 and multimodal reasoning
items.summary: Latent Space's AINews analysis argues GPT-Image-2's closed-loop integration with coding systems represents a qualitative leap toward AGI-level multimodal reasoning, not just better image generation. Examines how image generation benchmarks are becoming proxies for general reasoning capability.

items.title: Autonomous AI at Scale: Adobe Agents Unlock Creative Intelligence With NVIDIA and WPP
items.summary: Adobe, NVIDIA, and WPP deployed autonomous AI agents for creative marketing workflows, connecting Adobe's Firefly models with NVIDIA's inference infrastructure to generate, review, and adapt brand assets at scale.

items.title: microsoft/VibeVoice: open-source speech-to-text with built-in speaker diarization
items.summary: Microsoft released VibeVoice under MIT licence, a speech-to-text model with built-in speaker diarization. Simon Willison benchmarked the 4-bit quantised version transcribing an hour-long podcast in under 9 minutes on an M5 MacBook Pro.

items.title: Latent Space: Physical AI that moves the world — Applied Intuition on deploying AI to safety-critical systems
items.summary: Applied Intuition's Qasar Younis and Peter Ludwig discuss deploying AI to autonomous vehicles, mining equipment, and defence applications on the Latent Space podcast. Covers simulation-first development, domain-specific operating systems, and why safety-critical physical AI requires fundamentally different engineering from software agents.

items.title: Elon Musk boosts an X post by Ronan Farrow promoting his New Yorker article on Sam Altman's alleged deceptions, as Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI heads to trial (Wired)
items.summary: Wired : Elon Musk boosts an X post by Ronan Farrow promoting his New Yorker article on Sam Altman's alleged deceptions, as Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI heads to trial   —  The move comes as the trial for Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI kicks off in federal court in Oakland.

items.title: Jury selection complete in Musk v. Altman trial; opening arguments begin Tuesday in Oakland
items.summary: A nine-person jury was seated Monday in the federal Musk v. Altman trial in Oakland, California; both Sam Altman and Greg Brockman attended. Opening statements and witness testimony begin Tuesday. The civil case centres on whether OpenAI's shift to a for-profit structure breached its founding charter, with Musk, Altman, and possibly Satya Nadella expected to testify over four weeks.

items.title: NZ mid-sized firms lag on AI readiness, MYOB finds
items.summary: MYOB's annual readiness survey shows New Zealand's mid-market is investing in AI but trailing on workforce skills, governance, and process autonomy. Useful baseline for any NZ corporate AI conversation in 2026.

items.title: Tracking the history of the now-deceased OpenAI Microsoft AGI clause
items.summary: For many years, Microsoft and OpenAI's relationship has included a weird clause saying that, should AGI be achieved, Microsoft's commercial IP rights to OpenAI's technology would be null and void. That clause appeared to end today. I decided to try and track its expression over time on openai.com . OpenAI, July 22nd 2019 in Microsoft invests in and partners with OpenAI to support us building benef

items.title: Speech translation in Google Meet is now rolling out to mobile devices
items.summary: Speech translation in Google Meet is now rolling out to mobile devices I just encountered this feature via a "try this out now" prompt in a Google Meet meeting. It kind-of worked! This is Google's implementation of the ultimate sci-fi translation app, where two people can talk to each other in two separate languages and Meet translates from one to the other and - with a short delay - repeats the t

items.title: GitHub says all Copilot plans will move to usage-based billing on June 1, replacing premium requests with monthly GitHub AI Credits (Mario Rodriguez/The GitHub Blog)
items.summary: Mario Rodriguez / The GitHub Blog : GitHub says all Copilot plans will move to usage-based billing on June 1, replacing premium requests with monthly GitHub AI Credits   —  Starting June 1, your Copilot usage will consume GitHub AI Credits.  —  TL;DR: Today, we are announcing that all GitHub Copilot plans will transition to usage-based billing on June 1, 2026.

items.title: The EU unveils new proposals under the DMA aimed at opening up Android to rivals' AI services; Google says the measures are "unwarranted intervention" (Samuel Stolton/Bloomberg)
items.summary: Samuel Stolton / Bloomberg : The EU unveils new proposals under the DMA aimed at opening up Android to rivals' AI services; Google says the measures are “unwarranted intervention”   —  Google was targeted by European Union watchdogs who unveiled a slate of proposals aimed at prising open its Android ecosystem to rivals' AI services.

items.title: 600+ Google and DeepMind employees sign open letter demanding Pichai bar DOD from using Google AI for classified military work
items.summary: More than 600 Google and DeepMind employees signed a letter to CEO Sundar Pichai demanding he reject classified AI contracts with the US Department of Defense as Google negotiates to deploy Gemini in Pentagon settings. Over 100 DeepMind researchers separately sent an internal letter barring use of DeepMind research in weapons development or autonomous targeting.

items.title: The founder of car rental platform PocketOS says a Cursor agent using Claude Opus 4.6 accidentally deleted a production database while in a staging environment (Jer/@lifeof_jer)
items.summary: Jer / @lifeof_jer : The founder of car rental platform PocketOS says a Cursor agent using Claude Opus 4.6 accidentally deleted a production database while in a staging environment   —  An AI Agent Just Destroyed Our Production Data. It Confessed in Writing.

items.title: Andrew Ng: AI-native software engineering teams operate very differently
items.summary: Andrew Ng essay on how AI-native engineering teams collapse the engineer/PM ratio, why generalists win, and how coding agents shift bottlenecks to design, marketing, and legal. Useful frame for hotloop-style team design conversations.

items.title: Canva Magic Layers silently replaced 'Palestine' in user designs; company says issue is fixed
items.summary: Canva's newly launched Magic Layers feature automatically replaced the word 'Palestine' in user designs, sparking a viral backlash on X. Canva said it 'moved quickly to investigate and fix' the issue. The incident highlights risks of AI content moderation embedded directly in creative tools.

items.title: Ex-DeepMind Principal Scientist David Silver raises $1.1B seed at $5.1B valuation for Ineffable Intelligence
items.summary: David Silver, who led DeepMind's reinforcement learning team and co-created AlphaZero, has raised $1.1B at a $5.1B valuation for UK startup Ineffable Intelligence — the largest seed round in European history. The lab aims to build AI 'superlearners' that acquire knowledge without human data via RL. Backers include Sequoia, Lightspeed, NVIDIA, Google, DST Global, and the UK Sovereign AI Fund.

items.title: OpenAI available at FedRAMP Moderate
items.summary: OpenAI is available at FedRAMP Moderate authorization for ChatGPT Enterprise and the OpenAI API, enabling secure AI adoption for U.S. federal agencies.

items.title: Microsoft and OpenAI remove a clause that would have given Microsoft IP rights until OpenAI achieved "AGI"; Microsoft retains use of OpenAI's models until 2032 (Aaron Holmes/The Information)
items.summary: Aaron Holmes / The Information : Microsoft and OpenAI remove a clause that would have given Microsoft IP rights until OpenAI achieved “AGI”; Microsoft retains use of OpenAI's models until 2032   —  Microsoft and OpenAI amended the terms of their arrangement, allowing OpenAI to sell its models on competing cloud providers, the companies said on Monday.

items.title: Microsoft and OpenAI amend their deal to let OpenAI serve all its products across any cloud provider; Microsoft will no longer pay a revenue share to OpenAI (OpenAI)
items.summary: OpenAI : Microsoft and OpenAI amend their deal to let OpenAI serve all its products across any cloud provider; Microsoft will no longer pay a revenue share to OpenAI   —  Amended agreement provides long-term clarity.  —  The rapid pace of innovation requires us to continue to evolve our partnership to benefit our customers and both companies.

items.title: Join the new AI Agents Vibe Coding Course from Google and Kaggle
items.summary: Google and Kaggle launch a free intensive on building agents with Gemini and Vertex AI, scheduled for June 2026. Aimed at developers shifting from chat-completions to agentic workflows.

items.title: 1News: US vows crackdown on Chinese firms 'exploiting' AI models
items.summary: 1News reports US administration commitments to act against Chinese AI firms accused by Anthropic and others of model distillation. Follows DeepSeek V4 release; reframes the supply-chain governance debate from a NZ-Pacific viewpoint.

items.title: Open-source Dirac agent tops TerminalBench 2.0 at 65.2% using Gemini-3-flash-preview
items.summary: Community-built open-source agent Dirac scored 65.2% on TerminalBench 2.0 with Gemini-3-flash-preview, beating Google's official 47.8% and closed-source leader Junie CLI's 64.3%. The project includes steps to avoid the benchmark gaming recently documented by researchers. HN discussion at 663 points.

items.title: Mistral launches Workflows in public preview — automated multi-step AI pipelines for business operations
items.summary: Mistral AI released Workflows in public preview, a native orchestration layer enabling automated multi-step AI pipelines built on its models. Targets enterprise business process automation, competing directly with OpenAI's Symphony and Anthropic's agent frameworks.

items.title: China's NDRC orders Meta to cancel its $2B acquisition of AI agent startup Manus
items.summary: China's National Development and Reform Commission ordered Meta to unwind its $2B acquisition of Chinese-founded AI agent startup Manus, citing tech sovereignty concerns after a months-long probe. Co-founders have been banned from leaving China. The deal, announced in December 2025, had already been partially integrated into Meta AI.

items.title: RNZ on Musk vs Altman trial: ongoing power struggle for OpenAI
items.summary: RNZ frames the upcoming Musk vs Altman trial as the next public chapter of the OpenAI control fight, following the dismissal of fraud claims earlier this week. NZ pickup of the global story for domestic readers.

items.title: Lapsus$ hack of Mercor exposed 4TB of voice and biometric data from 40,000 AI contractors
items.summary: A new analysis of the April Mercor breach (via LiteLLM supply chain attack) details how Lapsus$ stole 4TB of biometric data — including voice samples and ID documents — from 40,000 AI data contractors serving OpenAI, Anthropic, and Meta. Six class-action lawsuits have been filed. Mercor disputes the claims.

items.title: Announcing our partnership with the Republic of Korea
items.summary: Google DeepMind announces a strategic AI partnership with the South Korean government covering research collaboration, AI safety standards, and Gemini deployment in Korean public services. Part of the wider sovereign-AI push by frontier labs in Asia.

items.title: The next phase of the Microsoft OpenAI partnership
items.summary: OpenAI and Microsoft formalise the next stage of their partnership: continued Azure compute commitment, expanded model access for Microsoft products, and revised IP arrangements. Signals continued tight coupling despite the Anthropic-Google deal.

items.title: Lancom joins AWS Anthropic reseller programme for Bedrock
items.summary: NZ-based Lancom signs on as an AWS reseller for Anthropic Claude via Amazon Bedrock, leaning on a security/compliance positioning for enterprise customers. First explicit NZ channel partner on the Anthropic-via-Bedrock path.

items.title: How to build scalable web apps with OpenAI's Privacy Filter
items.summary: Hugging Face publishes an integration guide for OpenAI's open-weight Privacy Filter PII redaction model in production web stacks. Covers latency tradeoffs, batching, and deployment on HF Inference Endpoints.

items.title: An open-source spec for orchestration: Symphony
items.summary: OpenAI publishes Symphony, an open spec for multi-agent orchestration designed for use with Codex and other agent runtimes. Defines schemas for agent handoff, tool routing, and execution traces — direct competitor surface to Anthropic MCP and Google A2A.

items.title: Choco automates food distribution with AI agents
items.summary: OpenAI customer story: Berlin-based Choco deploys agents on the OpenAI stack to automate order intake and supplier matching across European food distributors. Concrete agentic deployment in a non-tech vertical.

items.title: NZ media flags Anthropic Claude Mythos Preview as "too dangerous", urges domestic guardrails
items.summary: The Post frames Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview as a high-risk frontier release reaching New Zealand without local regulatory infrastructure. References the central-bank monitoring posture established last week and pushes for NZ-side governance rather than relying on US/AU regulators.

items.title: NZ MPs and AI in Parliament: which party uses it most, an MP running his own AI server, and Grok in Question Time
items.summary: The Post investigates how New Zealand MPs use AI tools day-to-day in Parliament. The piece names the party with the highest AI adoption, identifies an MP self-hosting an LLM, and documents Grok being used to formulate questions during Question Time. First substantive look at AI usage inside the Beehive.

items.title: NVIDIA and Partners Showcase the Future of AI-Driven Manufacturing at Hannover Messe 2026
items.summary: At Hannover Messe 2026, NVIDIA and industrial partners demonstrated AI-driven manufacturing including robotic assembly, predictive maintenance, and digital twin workflows powered by the Omniverse platform.
